intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Perfect. Now I'll execute the standard QC and clustering workflow on the GSM5746268 sample (the first PBMC sample in your data).

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import os

# Set the figure output directory
sc.settings.figdir = '.'

# Load the 10x triplet for GSM5746268
DATA_DIR = os.environ['DATA_DIR']
pre = "GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1"

# Read the three files
adata = sc.read_mtx(f"{DATA_DIR}/{pre}.matrix.mtx.gz").T  # mtx is genes×cells, transpose to cells×genes
adata.obs_names = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/{pre}.barcodes.tsv.gz", header=None)[0].values
adata.var_names = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/{pre}.features.tsv.gz", header=None, sep='\t')[1].values  # col 2 = gene symbols
adata.var_names_make_unique()

print(f"Loaded: {adata.n_obs} cells × {adata.n_vars} genes")
print(f"Sample: {pre}")

# 2. QC metrics — flag mitochondrial genes
adata.var['mt'] = adata.var_names.str.upper().str.startswith('MT-')
print(f"Mitochondrial genes detected: {adata.var['mt'].sum()}")

sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(adata, qc_vars=['mt'], percent_top=None, log1p=False, inplace=True)

# Quick look at pre-filter distributions
print(f"QC stats before filter:")
print(f"  n_genes_by_counts: {adata.obs['n_genes_by_counts'].min():.0f} – {adata.obs['n_genes_by_counts'].max():.0f}")
print(f"  total_counts: {adata.obs['total_counts'].min():.0f} – {adata.obs['total_counts'].max():.0f}")
print(f"  pct_counts_mt: {adata.obs['pct_counts_mt'].min():.1f} – {adata.obs['pct_counts_mt'].max():.1f}")
